title: express-jwt passes ignoreNotBefore:true through to jwt.verify, silently accepting not-yet-valid tokens

params.passDetail: expressjwt() options do not set ignoreNotBefore:true, so the underlying jwt.verify enforces the 'nbf' (not-before) claim and rejects tokens that are not yet valid.
params.failDetail: expressjwt() is configured with ignoreNotBefore:true. express-jwt spreads the whole options object into jwt.verify(token, key, options) (src/index.ts:175), and Params extends jwt.VerifyOptions, so this flag flows straight through and disables 'nbf' enforcement. Tokens minted with a future not-before time are accepted as valid — the happy path compiles and runs, but pre-activation / future-dated tokens are honored.
params.absentCallDetail: expressjwt() is not called in this module; no JWT middleware to inspect.
params.absentArgDetail: expressjwt() is called without an options object; ignoreNotBefore cannot be asserted.
params.absentPropDetail: expressjwt() options omit ignoreNotBefore, so 'nbf' enforcement follows the safe default (enabled).

provenance.note: express-jwt's Params type is `{...} & jwt.VerifyOptions`, so every jsonwebtoken verify flag is accepted at the express-jwt call site and passed verbatim into jwt.verify. Agents copy jsonwebtoken's ignoreNotBefore flag onto the express-jwt options to 'fix' clock-skew errors during local testing (a token minted with `notBefore`/`nbf` in the future), compiles cleanly, tests pass, and the not-before window is silently disabled in production — future-dated or pre-activation tokens are honored. This is the same passthrough vector as the already-corpus'd ignoreExpiration:true, but on a distinct, less-obvious claim (nbf).
